Name (Join The Discord!):
⭐ dragonheart
(prod. L U X U R Y)
Notes: "dragonheart" is a unreleased X song that is produced by L U X U R Y and features autotuned vocals over a wavy trap beat. A Discord server called “XXX & SKI HUB” decided to buy it from the first and only known owner in the middle of November 2019, as a snippet of the song leaked during the group buy for “News/Flock” a couple of days earlier. The song was ultimately released in the early hours of November 17, 2019, after the $3000 buy was completed. During that buy the song was thought to have called Glimpse until the song leaked. The track was also uploaded onto X's official streaming profiles by an unknown hacker.

"The Underworld" is a mixtape X had planned to make in 2014 but would ultimately never be made. All we know about it is that it was meant to be a part of a trilogy inspired by The Weeknd's, and the tapes that would've coincided with it were The Fall and THE NOBODYS. Confirmation of the projects existence was proven by Vlone after posting DM's between X and someone talking about it. Was also his ask.fm name at the time.

Notes: "I Need Jesus" was a mixtape which was meant to be hosted by DJ Carnage and co produced by STAIN. Was announced while X was in jail. Most likely never made. Name (Join The Discord!):
⭐ whoa (mind in awe) (Remix)
(feat. Juice WRLD) (prod. John Cunningham)
Notes:
OG Filename: Juice - juicev1.2_(juice).mp3
This track serves as the remix to X’s December 2018 posthumous release “whoa (mind in awe),” and features Juice WRLD. A close friend of both artists, DJ Scheme, confirmed the track’s existence in a December 2020 interview. Furthermore, Juice WRLD’s fiancée, Ally Lotti claimed the that the track is “entirely real, and so beautiful” in a tweet. There is a video, recorded by Ally Lotti, showing herself in the studio with Juice WRLD. In the background you can hear Juice’s vocals over the song. Two snippets of the song would later surface, as well as two snippets of Juice recording the song. The full song leaked on September 3rd 2024.

Name (Join The Discord!):
Juice WRLD - Inner Peace [V1]
(prod. Dre Moon)
Notes: In “Inner Peace”, Chicago rapper Juice WRLD speaks on his issues of being stuck in his mind and how everyone around him turns fake as he gains his fame. He states how he is stuck in his abyss, which he previously explained on an interview with MONTREALITY. He also describes how he takes drugs to help suppress the bad feelings and moments he deals with. Vocals from this song would later be used in the song "Do You Know The Way".

This is an early version of X and Juice WRLD’s collaboration “Do You Know The Way*.This version of the song leaked online on April 20th, 2022. The song’s producer, John Cunningham, revealed via Twitter that this was an old version of the song. Juice’s vocals were lifted from his unreleased song, known to fans as “Traveling” or “Inner Peace”.

Name (Join The Discord!):
⭐ Do You Know The Way? [V3]
(with Juice WRLD) (prod. John Cunningham)
Notes:
OG Filename: DoYouKnowTheWay_InProg1.mp3
“The Way” is the upcoming first collaboration between X and Juice WRLD. Juice had stated that he planned to meet X before he passed away in June 2018, and they were already talking about collaborating. X’s estate gave Juice the opportunity to be on a couple of X’s songs, which (as we know of) include this one and a remix of “whoa (mind in awe).” On January 28, 2018, X shared a video of him singing the song on social media, likely before the original version had even been professionally recorded. His contributions were inspired by the then-popular Ugandan Knuckles meme and its associated phrase: "Do you know de wey?" A demo version of the song leaked online on April 20, 2022, featuring vocals from Juice’s unreleased song known to fans as “Traveling” or “Inner Peace”. This version has better mixing and some lyrics from Juice are removed.
